title: fraxinus excelsior subsp. parvifolia
intro: Fraxinus excelsior subsp. parvifolia, also known as the small-leaved ash, is a subspecies of the European ash tree, characterized by its smaller leaves compared to the typical Fraxinus excelsior. The bark and leaves of this plant have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Fraxinus excelsior subsp. parvifolia include relief from fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its use as a natural diuretic. Fraxinus excelsior subsp. parvifolia can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures for its medicinal uses.
